channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Save the Children Fall 2026 Internship Program Opens: A Powerful Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates to Build Careers While Changing Children’s Lives
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Students and recent graduates looking to gain real-world experience while making a meaningful global impact now have an exciting opportunity to launch their careers through the Fall 2026 Internship Program by Save the Children and Save the Children Action Network. The globally recognized humanitarian organization has officially announced the return of its highly anticipated 11-week […]

The post Save the Children Fall 2026 Internship Program Opens: A Powerful Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates to Build Careers While Changing Children’s Lives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and recent graduates looking to gain real-world experience while making a meaningful global impact now have an exciting opportunity to launch their careers through the Fall 2026 Internship Program by Save the Children and Save the Children Action Network.

The globally recognized humanitarian organization has officially announced the return of its highly anticipated 11-week internship program, welcoming a new cohort of emerging professionals passionate about creating change for children and communities across the world.

Designed for college students, graduate students, and recent graduates, the internship program offers participants the opportunity to gain hands-on professional experience, develop practical workplace skills, and build valuable networks within one of the world’s leading nonprofit organizations.

For young professionals seeking a career pathway in humanitarian work, advocacy, public policy, nonprofit leadership, international development, communications, research, or social impact, this internship presents a rare opportunity to learn directly from experienced leaders while contributing to real projects that improve children’s lives in the United States and globally.

Why the Save the Children Internship Program Matters

Internship opportunities are becoming increasingly important for students and graduates seeking competitive career advantages in today’s job market. However, programs that combine career development with genuine social impact remain limited.

The Save the Children Fall 2026 Internship Program stands out because it goes beyond traditional internship experiences. Rather than simply assigning administrative tasks, the program is structured to actively engage interns in meaningful work that supports children’s education, health, safety, and future opportunities.

According to the organization, the internship program serves as one of its strongest tools for mentoring and nurturing the next generation of leaders committed to humanitarian service and global development.

Interns are not only given practical exposure to nonprofit operations, but they are also immersed in a mission-driven environment where their contributions can directly support vulnerable children and communities.

What Interns Can Expect from the 11-Week Program

Participants selected for the Fall 2026 cohort can expect a comprehensive professional development experience that combines learning, mentorship, collaboration, and impact-driven work.

Key benefits of the program include:

1. Hands-On Professional Experience

Interns will work alongside experienced professionals on projects connected to child welfare, advocacy campaigns, humanitarian programs, communications initiatives, policy work, and organizational operations.

This practical exposure helps interns bridge the gap between academic learning and professional execution.

2. Career Skill Development

The program is designed to strengthen essential workplace skills, including:

  • Professional communication
  • Team collaboration
  • Research and analysis
  • Leadership development
  • Strategic thinking
  • Advocacy and policy understanding
  • Project coordination
  • Networking and relationship building

These skills are valuable across nonprofit, corporate, government, and international development careers.

3. Networking Opportunities

One of the biggest advantages of the internship is access to professionals, mentors, and peers working within the humanitarian and nonprofit sectors.

Interns can build relationships that may support future employment, mentorship, and career growth opportunities.

4. Meaningful Global Impact

Unlike many traditional internships, this program allows participants to contribute to initiatives that help children survive, learn, and stay protected both in the U.S. and internationally.

For purpose-driven students and graduates, this creates a more fulfilling and impactful internship experience.

Who Should Apply?

The Save the Children Fall 2026 Internship Program is ideal for:

  • Students passionate about social impact
  • Graduates interested in nonprofit careers
  • Future policymakers and advocates
  • Communications and media students
  • International development enthusiasts
  • Public health and education students
  • Young professionals interested in humanitarian leadership

Candidates who are passionate about creating opportunities for children and contributing to global change are especially encouraged to apply.

Why This Internship Could Be a Career-Changing Opportunity

In today’s competitive employment environment, employers increasingly seek candidates who possess not only academic qualifications but also practical experience, leadership ability, and social awareness.

Completing an internship with a globally respected organization like Save the Children can significantly strengthen a candidate’s professional profile.

The experience demonstrates:

  • Commitment to service
  • Professional initiative
  • Global awareness
  • Real-world problem-solving abilities
  • Adaptability in mission-driven environments

Additionally, interns gain insight into how international nonprofits operate, advocate for policy change, mobilize humanitarian support, and implement programs that affect millions of children worldwide.

Application Information

Interested students and recent graduates are encouraged to explore available internship opportunities and submit their applications through the official Save the Children careers platform.

Applicants should prepare:

  • An updated resume or CV
  • Relevant academic and professional information
  • A strong statement of interest or cover letter where required
  • Supporting documents requested for individual internship roles

Because opportunities within globally recognized organizations are highly competitive, early application is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can explore available opportunities through the official careers page:

If you are passionate about improving children’s lives, building a meaningful career, and becoming part of the next generation of global changemakers, this internship opportunity could be the perfect step forward in your professional journey.

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY here

The post Save the Children Fall 2026 Internship Program Opens: A Powerful Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates to Build Careers While Changing Children’s Lives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google Student Ambassador Program 2026 in USA (Paid Remote Opportunity for Undergraduate Students)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Details Host Organization: Google Program Name: Google Student Ambassador Program 2026 Location: Remote (United States) Eligible Applicants: Full-time undergraduate students in the USA Application Deadline: June 1, 2026 Program Duration: August/September – December 2026 Time Commitment: Up to 15 hours per week Benefits: Paid opportunity, Google mentorship, leadership experience, networking, exclusive Google access, and […]

The post Google Student Ambassador Program 2026 in USA (Paid Remote Opportunity for Undergraduate Students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Host Organization: Google
  • Program Name: Google Student Ambassador Program 2026
  • Location: Remote (United States)
  • Eligible Applicants: Full-time undergraduate students in the USA
  • Application Deadline: June 1, 2026
  • Program Duration: August/September – December 2026
  • Time Commitment: Up to 15 hours per week
  • Benefits: Paid opportunity, Google mentorship, leadership experience, networking, exclusive Google access, and ambassador swag
  • Focus Areas: Artificial Intelligence, Gemini AI, student leadership, content creation, campus engagement, and technology advocacy

To participate in the Google Student Ambassador program, you must be located in the United States and have valid legal work authorization for the duration of the program. This is a remote paid position. If your status requires Curricular Practical Training (CPT) authorization, please consult with your school to confirm your eligibility to participate in this program before applying.

While this is a remote paid position, Ambassadors are required to be physically present on their currently enrolled university or college campus throughout the program.

Google is a global company and, in order to facilitate efficient collaboration and communication globally, English proficiency is a requirement for this Ambassador program.

All fields of study are encouraged to apply.

To start the application process, you will need an updated CV or resume and a current unofficial or official transcript in English. Click on the “Apply” button on this page and provide the required materials in the appropriate sections (PDFs preferred).

1. In the “Resume Section:” attach an updated CV or resume.

2. In the “Education Section:” attach a current or recent unofficial or official transcript in English.

  • Under “Degree Status,” select “Now attending” to upload a transcript.

Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

Applicants in the County of Los Angeles: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

Note: Google’s hybrid workplace includes remote roles. By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following:

Remote locations: California, USA; United States.

Minimum qualifications:

  • Currently enrolled as a full-time undergraduate student at an accredited university in the U.S.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience in event planning or workshop facilitation including public speaking, presentation, and people management skills, with the ability to be comfortable hosting groups both online and in-person.
  • Comfort and familiarity with social media platforms and posting regular content.
  • Ability to work independently, manage a flexible schedule, and take creative initiative to meet program goals.
  • Active in campus clubs, organizations, or leadership roles with an authentic network among peers.
  • Passion for AI, machine learning, technology, and familiarity with Gemini app.

About the job

Lead the Google AI Movement on Your Campus!

Are you fascinated by the power of generative AI? Do you love sharing new tech and helping your peers find innovative ways to learn and create? Google is looking for passionate, dynamic, and connected student leaders to be the face of Google’s AI tools on their college campus.

This is more than just a resume builder—it’s an opportunity to gain exclusive access, receive mentorship from Googlers, and lead the AI conversation that is redefining the future of learning and work.

As a Google Student Ambassador, you will be the primary on-campus liaison and advocate for Google’s AI products. Your main focus will be on driving awareness and usage of our AI tools like the Gemini app to help students in their academic and personal lives.

The Details

  • Cohort Timeline: August/September – December 2026
  • Time Commitment: Up to 15 hours per week for the full duration of the program. (Note: Detailed time commitment on a daily basis may fluctuate based on seasonalities / activities.)
  • No conflicting employment affiliations with other AI platforms.
  • Location: Your university or college campus while classes are in session

Program Benefits & Perks

  • Hourly Pay
  • Resume Boost: A highly distinguished line on your resume that demonstrates leadership, marketing, and tech advocacy for one of the world’s biggest brands.
  • Exclusive Google Access: Gain unique insights and early access to new Google AI tools & features, learn from experts, and share your knowledge with your campus.
  • Recognition & Swag: Receive exclusive Google-branded Ambassador gear and opportunities for community spotlights and special recognition based on your contributions.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $46,000-$61,500. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.

Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google.

Responsibilities

  • Organize and lead engaging on-campus events, such as AI-powered study sessions and demos, “Prompt Battles,” content creation jams, and tabling events. Establish and manage a dedicated campus community (e.g., club, online group, or discord channel) where peers can share tips, ask questions, and collaborate on Gemini projects.
  • Conduct regular, informal learning sessions demonstrating practical applications of Google AI products for tasks like exam prep, summarizing research, generating code snippets, and sparking creative ideas. Develop and share authentic, engaging content (e.g., short videos, blog posts, social media takeovers) that showcase how Google’s AI tools are relevant to student life and academic success.
  • Utilize your existing networks within student organizations, academic departments, and campus media to promote Google AI events and resources. Act as the main point of contact between Google and your university community, sharing the latest product updates, news, and opportunities.
  • Gather and synthesize genuine student feedback, observations, and use-cases to help Google’s product and marketing teams improve the student experience. Provide weekly or monthly reports on event attendance, social media engagement, and community growth metrics.
  • Participate on research and insight panels with Google teams to inform product positioning and strategy.

APPLY NOW

CLICK HERE FOR MORE RELATED OPPORTUNITIES BY GOOGLE WORLDWIDE

Information collected and processed as part of your Google Careers profile, and any job applications that you choose to submit are subject to Google’s applicant and candidate privacy policy.

Google is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to building a workforce that is representative of the users we serve, creating a culture of belonging and providing an equal employment opportunity regardless of race, creed, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), expecting or parents-to-be, criminal histories consistent with legal requirements or any other basis protected by law. See also Google’s EEO Policy, Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal, Belonging at Google and How we hire.

If you have a need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Google is a global company and, in order to facilitate efficient collaboration and communication globally, English proficiency is a requirement for all roles unless stated otherwise in the job posting.

To all recruitment agencies: Google does not accept agency CVs. Please do not forward CVs to our jobs alias, Google employees or any other organisation location. Google is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited CVs.

APPLY NOW

FOR MROE INFO PLEASE CLICK HERE 

CLICK HERE FOR MORE RELATED OPPORTUNITIES BY GOOGLE  WORLDWIDE

The post Google Student Ambassador Program 2026 in USA (Paid Remote Opportunity for Undergraduate Students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students Across Commonwealth Countries
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027 are now open for applications, offering exceptional funding opportunities for PhD students conducting research on Commonwealth-related themes. Supported by The Round Table: The Commonwealth Journal of International Affairs and Policy Studies, Routledge, and the Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU), this prestigious programme aims to strengthen scholarly research, international […]

The post Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students Across Commonwealth Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027 are now open for applications, offering exceptional funding opportunities for PhD students conducting research on Commonwealth-related themes. Supported by The Round Table: The Commonwealth Journal of International Affairs and Policy Studies, Routledge, and the Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU), this prestigious programme aims to strengthen scholarly research, international collaboration, and policy development across the Commonwealth.

The studentship programme supports doctoral candidates whose work contributes to understanding the Commonwealth, comparative studies among Commonwealth nations, or issues affecting multiple Commonwealth countries. Each year, two studentships valued at up to £5,500 GBP each are awarded to outstanding PhD researchers.

This opportunity is ideal for ambitious doctoral students seeking financial support, international mentorship, research exposure, publication opportunities, and professional networking within the Commonwealth academic community.

Overview of the Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards

The studentship programme was established to encourage innovative and impactful research focusing on Commonwealth affairs, international development, governance, policy, politics, social issues, education, law, and related fields.

Two awards are available annually:

  1. One studentship for a PhD student registered at a UK university
  2. One studentship for a PhD student registered at a university in another Commonwealth country

Each award has a maximum value of £5,500 GBP.

The programme particularly encourages interdisciplinary research and comparative studies that connect multiple Commonwealth nations or institutions.

Financial Benefits of the Studentship

Successful applicants will receive funding support of up to £5,500 GBP.

Breakdown of Funding

  • £4,000 GBP is paid immediately upon announcement of the awards
  • £1,500 GBP is paid after successful completion of post-studentship requirements

Additional Mentor Support

Mentors participating in the programme receive an honorarium of £500 GBP after the studentship period.

Fields of Study Eligible for the Award

Applications are welcomed from a broad range of disciplines within the humanities and social sciences.

Preferred areas include:

  • Politics
  • International Relations
  • Economics
  • International History
  • Geography
  • Law
  • Development Studies
  • Area Studies
  • Public Policy
  • Sociology
  • Governance
  • Education Policy
  • Media and Communication

Applications in pure sciences and medicine are generally not eligible. However, research relating to science policy, healthcare policy, or public health governance may still be considered.

Research Themes That Qualify

To qualify for the studentship, proposed research must meet at least one of the following criteria:

  • Relate to the Commonwealth as a whole
  • Focus on Commonwealth-wide institutions or organisations
  • Include a comparative Commonwealth aspect
  • Address issues affecting more than one Commonwealth country

Examples of Eligible Research

  • Comparative education systems in Commonwealth countries
  • Human rights across Commonwealth nations
  • Climate governance and sustainability policies
  • Health policy and healthcare systems
  • Digital governance and cybersecurity
  • Democracy, elections, and governance reforms
  • Colonial legacies and postcolonial development
  • International diplomacy and foreign policy
  • Migration and development studies

The programme also welcomes applications from students whose broader PhD work may not initially focus on the Commonwealth but where the award could help incorporate a Commonwealth dimension into the research.

Eligibility Requirements

For UK-Based Students

Applicants must:

  • Be registered PhD students at UK universities
  • Not necessarily be UK citizens

For Students Outside the UK

Applicants must:

  • Be registered at ACU member universities in Commonwealth countries outside the UK
  • Or be Chevening or Commonwealth Scholarship alumni enrolled at Commonwealth universities outside the UK

Mentorship Requirement

A unique and important component of the programme is the mentorship structure.

Applicants must identify a mentor located in another Commonwealth country different from their own institution.

Role of the Mentor

The mentor should:

  • Be an expert connected to the applicant’s research area
  • Provide academic guidance and support
  • Hold at least two virtual meetings during the studentship
  • Offer advice on publishing, networking, and professional development
  • Participate in either the final podcast or article submission

Applicants are encouraged to contact potential mentors informally before submitting their application.

Post-Studentship Requirements

Award recipients are expected to contribute scholarly outputs after receiving the studentship.

Within twelve months of receiving the award, students must submit:

1. Research Article

A scholarly article between 4,000 and 6,000 words based on their research for consideration in The Round Table journal.

2. Research Podcast

A podcast discussing their research findings for publication on the journal’s official website.

These requirements help students build publication experience and strengthen their academic visibility internationally.

Why This Studentship Is Important

The Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards offer more than financial assistance. The programme creates opportunities for doctoral researchers to:

  • Expand comparative international research
  • Build academic collaborations across Commonwealth countries
  • Gain mentorship from experienced scholars
  • Improve publication and communication skills
  • Strengthen policy-oriented research impact
  • Access wider academic and professional networks

The programme is especially valuable for students interested in governance, development, social justice, education, diplomacy, public policy, and global affairs.

Application Process

Applications for the 2026/2027 awards are now officially open.

How to Apply

Applicants must:

  1. Download the official application form
  2. Complete all required sections
  3. Submit the form via email to: secretary@commonwealthroundtable.co.uk 

Application Deadline

The closing date for applications is: 31 July 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to allow sufficient time for securing mentorship arrangements and preparing supporting materials.

Selection and Announcement

Successful candidates are expected to be announced in September 2026.

The selection process focuses on:

  • Academic quality
  • Commonwealth relevance
  • Research impact
  • Comparative significance
  • Feasibility and originality of the proposal

Examples of Previous Award Winners

The programme has supported many outstanding doctoral researchers from across the Commonwealth.

Past winners have conducted research on:

  • Artificial intelligence and counterterrorism in Africa
  • Educational inequality in India
  • Climate and naval security
  • Healthcare governance
  • Democracy and political systems
  • Media narratives and foreign policy
  • Public health and dementia policy
  • Agricultural resilience and mental wellbeing

These examples demonstrate the wide scope and international significance of projects supported by the studentship.

Official Links

Application information and programme details can be found through The Round Table and Association of Commonwealth Universities official platforms.

Prospective applicants should carefully review all eligibility and mentorship requirements before submitting their applications.

Final Thoughts

The Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027 represent a remarkable opportunity for PhD students seeking to strengthen their research through international collaboration and Commonwealth-focused scholarship.

With funding support, expert mentorship, publication opportunities, and global academic visibility, the programme empowers doctoral researchers to contribute meaningful solutions to issues affecting Commonwealth nations and communities.

Students with innovative ideas and a passion for impactful research are encouraged to apply before the 31 July 2026 deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Routledge/Round Table Commonwealth Studentship Awards 2026/2027: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students Across Commonwealth Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Creators to Inspire Change Through Film
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026 is now officially accepting submissions from young filmmakers, storytellers, activists, students, and creative visionaries from around the world. Organized by the United Nations Alliance of Civilizations (UNAOC), the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival is one of the world’s leading international youth video competitions focused on migration, diversity, social inclusion, intercultural […]

The post PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Creators to Inspire Change Through Film appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026 is now officially accepting submissions from young filmmakers, storytellers, activists, students, and creative visionaries from around the world. Organized by the United Nations Alliance of Civilizations (UNAOC), the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival is one of the world’s leading international youth video competitions focused on migration, diversity, social inclusion, intercultural dialogue, and positive social transformation.

This prestigious global initiative empowers young people to use the power of film and digital storytelling to challenge stereotypes, promote inclusion, encourage empathy, and amplify voices that are often underrepresented in mainstream media.

Each year, PLURAL+ receives inspiring video submissions from youth across continents who are passionate about creating a more inclusive and understanding world. The festival serves as a global platform where young creators can showcase their talents while addressing some of the most important social issues affecting humanity today.

If you are a young creative with a powerful story to tell, the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026 offers an incredible opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to meaningful social change through visual storytelling.

What Is the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival?

PLURAL+ is a youth-focused video festival launched by the United Nations Alliance of Civilizations (UNAOC) in partnership with international organizations, media institutions, educators, and youth networks worldwide.

The initiative encourages youth to produce short videos that explore themes such as:

  • Migration
  • Diversity
  • Social inclusion
  • Identity
  • Human rights
  • Refugee experiences
  • Cultural understanding
  • Peacebuilding
  • Anti-discrimination
  • Community integration
  • Intercultural dialogue

PLURAL+ recognizes that young people are among the most powerful agents of change in society. Through creative media and storytelling, participants are encouraged to challenge harmful narratives and inspire constructive global conversations.

Why the PLURAL+ Festival Matters

In a world increasingly shaped by migration, globalization, and cultural interaction, conversations around diversity and inclusion are more important than ever.

PLURAL+ creates a platform where youth perspectives are not only welcomed but celebrated. The festival highlights the ability of film and storytelling to:

  • Build empathy across communities
  • Reduce prejudice and discrimination
  • Encourage peaceful coexistence
  • Promote social cohesion
  • Amplify marginalized voices
  • Inspire policy discussions and public awareness
  • Foster intercultural understanding

By participating, young creators become part of a worldwide movement advocating for positive social impact through media.

Benefits of Participating in PLURAL+ 2026

The PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival offers participants much more than a traditional competition experience.

Key Benefits Include:

1. International Recognition

Winning or being recognized by PLURAL+ can significantly elevate a young creator’s profile globally. Selected videos are showcased internationally and promoted through UNAOC and partner networks.

2. Global Exposure

Participants gain visibility among international audiences, media organizations, educators, policymakers, NGOs, and film professionals.

3. Professional Opportunities

PLURAL+ recipients may receive opportunities for:

  • International screenings
  • Media exposure
  • Festival participation
  • Networking opportunities
  • Educational partnerships

4. Amplifying Important Messages

Creators have the opportunity to spark conversations on critical social issues and influence positive societal change through storytelling.

5. Personal and Creative Growth

Participants develop:

  • Filmmaking skills
  • Storytelling techniques
  • Social awareness
  • Advocacy communication skills
  • International collaboration opportunities

Themes for Video Submissions

Videos submitted to PLURAL+ should focus on one or more of the festival’s core themes.

Accepted Themes Include:

  • Migration stories and experiences
  • Refugee rights and inclusion
  • Diversity and multiculturalism
  • Social integration
  • Youth empowerment
  • Combating racism and xenophobia
  • Human dignity and equality
  • Intercultural friendship and dialogue
  • Positive community engagement
  • Peace and tolerance

Participants are encouraged to create original, authentic, and impactful narratives that inspire audiences and promote understanding.

Who Can Apply?

The competition is open to youth worldwide regardless of nationality, ethnicity, religion, or background.

Eligible participants include:

  • Students
  • Young filmmakers
  • Youth organizations
  • Activists
  • Digital storytellers
  • Independent creators
  • Social advocates
  • Emerging media professionals

PLURAL+ strongly values diversity and inclusion, making it accessible to participants from all regions of the world.

Video Submission Guidelines

Participants are required to submit original videos aligned with the festival themes and regulations.

Videos should:

  • Promote positive social change
  • Address migration, diversity, or inclusion
  • Reflect authentic youth perspectives
  • Demonstrate creativity and originality
  • Follow official submission rules and technical requirements

Applicants are advised to review the official rules and regulations before submission.

Deadline for Submission

The official submission deadline for the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026 is: 30 June 2026

Early submissions are strongly encouraged to avoid technical or administrative delays.

How to Submit Your Video

To participate in the PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026:

  1. Visit the official PLURAL+ website
  2. Review the festival rules and FAQs
  3. Complete the online entry form
  4. Upload your original video submission
  5. Submit before the deadline

Apply Here

About the United Nations Alliance of Civilizations (UNAOC)

The United Nations Alliance of Civilizations is an initiative dedicated to improving understanding and cooperation among nations, cultures, and communities.

Through PLURAL+, UNAOC supports youth engagement, intercultural dialogue, peacebuilding, and social inclusion by empowering young people to share their voices through creative media.

The initiative collaborates with numerous global media organizations, educational institutions, and international partners to maximize the reach and impact of youth storytelling.

Why You Should Apply

The PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival is more than just a film competition. It is a global platform for youth leadership, creative advocacy, and social impact.

By participating, young creators can:

  • Gain international recognition
  • Build a professional portfolio
  • Inspire global audiences
  • Advocate for social justice
  • Connect with like-minded changemakers
  • Share powerful untold stories

Whether you are an experienced filmmaker or a first-time storyteller, PLURAL+ welcomes authentic voices that seek to make the world more inclusive and compassionate.

Final Thoughts

The PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026 is an extraordinary opportunity for young people worldwide to use creativity and storytelling as tools for positive change.

As conversations around migration, diversity, and social inclusion continue to shape global societies, youth voices remain essential in building understanding, empathy, and peace.

If you have a meaningful story to tell, this is your chance to share it with the world.

Submit your video before 30 June 2026 and become part of a global movement using film to transform perspectives and inspire inclusion.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post PLURAL+ Youth Video Festival 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Creators to Inspire Change Through Film appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Monetary Fund (IMF) Research Analyst Program 2026 in Washington, DC: Fully Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has announced applications for its prestigious Research Analyst Program (RAP), an exceptional global career opportunity designed for highly talented and motivated recent graduates who are passionate about economics, finance, statistics, data science, and quantitative research. This highly competitive program gives young professionals the chance to work directly at the IMF […]

The post International Monetary Fund (IMF) Research Analyst Program 2026 in Washington, DC: Fully Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has announced applications for its prestigious Research Analyst Program (RAP), an exceptional global career opportunity designed for highly talented and motivated recent graduates who are passionate about economics, finance, statistics, data science, and quantitative research.

This highly competitive program gives young professionals the chance to work directly at the IMF Headquarters in Washington, D.C., where they will support major global economic research and policy projects while gaining world-class professional experience before pursuing graduate studies or long-term careers in international development and finance.

The IMF Research Analyst Program is particularly attractive for candidates who aspire to build careers in economics, macroeconomic policy, financial analysis, international development, data science, and global research institutions.

The opportunity is open to applicants from all regions of the world, making it one of the most accessible and internationally diverse research programs available to young graduates.

About the International Monetary Fund (IMF)

The International Monetary Fund is one of the world’s leading international financial institutions, dedicated to promoting global monetary cooperation, financial stability, sustainable economic growth, and poverty reduction across countries worldwide.

Working at the IMF provides exposure to international economic policy discussions, global financial systems, and high-level research that influences economic decisions across governments and institutions worldwide.

The IMF’s multicultural and globally diverse environment allows participants to collaborate with economists, researchers, analysts, and policy experts from different countries and academic backgrounds.

Overview of the Research Analyst Program (RAP)

The Research Analyst Program (RAP) is specifically designed for recent graduates who possess strong quantitative, analytical, and technical abilities.

The program offers participants a unique entry-level professional experience where they contribute directly to the operational and policy work of IMF economists.

The RAP appointment is limited to a two-year contractual period, allowing participants to gain substantial professional and research experience before moving on to graduate education or advanced professional careers.

All selected candidates will work at the IMF Headquarters located in downtown Washington, D.C.

What Research Analysts Will Do

Participants selected for the IMF Research Analyst Program will support major economic and financial research activities within the organization.

Their responsibilities may include:

Research and Economic Analysis

  • Conducting economic and quantitative research
  • Supporting operational and policy-related projects
  • Collaborating with IMF economists and researchers
  • Staying updated on developments in economics and finance

Data Collection and Management

  • Collecting and compiling macroeconomic data
  • Managing and updating financial and statistical databases
  • Accessing information from multiple international data sources
  • Ensuring data accuracy and integrity

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis

  • Performing econometric and statistical analyses
  • Evaluating economic and financial relationships in databases
  • Analyzing economic time-series data
  • Using quantitative tools to generate insights

Technical and Programming Tasks

  • Designing systems for data transfer and interfaces
  • Working with analytical software and programming languages
  • Supporting data visualization and reporting projects

Benefits of the IMF Research Analyst Program

The IMF Research Analyst Program offers several outstanding professional and career-development benefits for participants.

Key Benefits Include:

  1. Opportunity to work at the IMF Headquarters in Washington, D.C.
  2. Exposure to international economic and financial policy work
  3. Direct collaboration with global economists and policy experts
  4. Hands-on experience in economic and quantitative research
  5. Development of advanced analytical and technical skills
  6. Excellent preparation for graduate studies and PhD applications
  7. Valuable international professional networking opportunities
  8. Experience that may satisfy PREDOC requirements for future academic studies
  9. Multicultural and globally diverse working environment
  10. Strong career foundation in economics, finance, data science, and policy analysis

Minimum Eligibility Requirements

Applicants interested in the IMF Research Analyst Program must meet specific academic and technical qualifications.

Educational Requirements

Candidates must have recently completed a bachelor’s degree in one of the following fields:

  • Economics
  • Statistics
  • Mathematics
  • Computer Science
  • Finance
  • Data Science
  • Other related quantitative disciplines

Applicants with advanced degrees in relevant fields may also be considered if they possess limited combined professional experience.

GPA Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate strong academic performance through:

  • A cumulative GPA of at least 3.5/4.0
  • Or equivalent international academic performance standards

The GPA requirement applies both to:

  • The principal field of study
  • The overall academic program

Technical Skills and Competencies

The IMF is looking for highly analytical candidates with strong technical and quantitative capabilities.

Required Skills

Applicants should possess:

  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills
  • Ability to work independently
  • Attention to detail and accuracy
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English

Software and Technical Knowledge

Preferred technical competencies include:

  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • STATA
  • MATLAB
  • EViews
  • Statistical analysis tools

Additional Advantageous Skills

Knowledge in the following areas is considered highly desirable:

  • Python
  • R programming
  • Tableau
  • PowerBI
  • Data visualization tools
  • Web design

Proficiency in languages other than English is also considered an added advantage.

Application Instructions

Candidates applying for the IMF Research Analyst Program must carefully prepare and submit all required application documents.

Required Documents

Applicants must upload:

  1. Resume or CV
  2. Cover Letter
  3. Official or unofficial academic transcript

The transcript must clearly verify:

  • Current or final GPA
  • Academic performance details

The IMF notes that applications submitted without proof of GPA or academic achievement may not be considered.

Important Selection Information

  • Applications are welcomed from all geographical regions
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted
  • Selected candidates will discuss assignment interests and availability directly with hiring managers
  • The IMF promotes diversity and equal opportunity in recruitment and employment

The IMF also welcomes requests for reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities during the selection process.

Why You Should Apply

For recent graduates aiming to build careers in economics, international finance, policy research, or data analytics, the IMF Research Analyst Program represents a rare and prestigious global opportunity.

The program combines professional development, international exposure, technical training, and high-level research experience within one of the most influential financial institutions in the world.

Whether your long-term goal is graduate school, international development, central banking, policy analysis, or economic research, the IMF RAP can provide the skills, network, and professional experience needed to accelerate your career.

Application Deadline

No official application deadline has been specified in the announcement. Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Official Application Link

Interested applicants can apply through the official IMF careers portal and LinkedIn job announcement page.

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post International Monetary Fund (IMF) Research Analyst Program 2026 in Washington, DC: Fully Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Young professionals working in heritage conservation, historic gardens, architecture, landscape design, and cultural preservation now have an exciting global opportunity to showcase their work through the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme in Suzhou, China. Organized by the UNESCO Regional Office for East Asia and the World Heritage Institute of Training and Research […]

The post UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young professionals working in heritage conservation, historic gardens, architecture, landscape design, and cultural preservation now have an exciting global opportunity to showcase their work through the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme in Suzhou, China.

Organized by the UNESCO Regional Office for East Asia and the World Heritage Institute of Training and Research for the Asia-Pacific Region (WHITR-AP Suzhou), this prestigious programme is calling for innovative, youth-led projects focused on historic gardens, living heritage conservation, sustainability, and public engagement.

The 2026 edition is themed:

“Historic Gardens: Living Heritage Conservation and Public Engagement.”

Selected participants will participate in an exclusive regional workshop in Suzhou, China, where they will engage in interdisciplinary dialogue, present their work, exchange knowledge with experts, and contribute to regional discussions on heritage conservation and sustainable cultural development.

This fully funded opportunity is designed to empower young professionals and practitioners who are actively preserving cultural landscapes while integrating sustainability, biodiversity conservation, traditional craftsmanship, and community engagement into their projects.

Applications are open until June 30, 2026.

About the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation Programme

The Young Masters for Heritage Conservation initiative recognizes that youth are critical custodians of cultural and natural heritage. Historic gardens, as living heritage systems, represent a unique intersection of architecture, nature, artistry, culture, and history.

The programme aims to:

  1. Recognize outstanding youth-led contributions to heritage conservation
  2. Promote innovative approaches to preserving historic gardens
  3. Encourage sustainable heritage management practices
  4. Foster regional collaboration and knowledge exchange
  5. Strengthen public engagement with cultural landscapes
  6. Connect heritage conservation with biodiversity and climate resilience
  7. Build a network of emerging heritage professionals across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond

The initiative particularly values projects that combine traditional conservation approaches with contemporary innovation and sustainability.

Why Historic Gardens Matter in Heritage Conservation

Historic gardens are more than beautiful landscapes. They are living expressions of culture, identity, craftsmanship, and ecological wisdom.

These heritage spaces:

  • Reflect the relationship between humans and nature
  • Preserve traditional architectural and horticultural knowledge
  • Support biodiversity and environmental sustainability
  • Serve as cultural gathering spaces
  • Promote tourism, education, and community engagement
  • Contribute to climate resilience and urban sustainability

UNESCO’s programme seeks to preserve these important cultural landscapes while making them relevant and accessible to modern society.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected participants will receive several professional and academic benefits through the programme.

1. Fully Funded Participation in the Suzhou Workshop

Participants selected for the workshop will have major costs covered, including:

  • International travel expenses
  • Accommodation in Suzhou
  • Meals during the programme
  • Local transportation
  • Insurance coverage

This makes the programme highly accessible for young professionals worldwide.

2. International Professional Exposure

Participants will gain visibility within UNESCO’s international heritage network and connect with:

  • Heritage conservation experts
  • Architects and landscape designers
  • Cultural practitioners
  • Policy experts
  • Conservation managers
  • Researchers and academics

3. Knowledge Exchange and Peer Learning

The workshop will provide opportunities for:

  • Collaborative learning
  • Interdisciplinary discussions
  • Sharing innovative conservation models
  • Exploring regional and global heritage challenges
  • Learning sustainable conservation strategies

4. Certificate of Recognition

Participants who complete the programme will receive a:

“Young Masters for Heritage Conservation” Certificate

This certificate can significantly strengthen professional profiles in architecture, heritage conservation, cultural management, and urban sustainability.

5. Exhibition and Project Showcase

Participants’ work may be featured in curated exhibitions showcasing innovative heritage conservation practices.

6. Global Networking Opportunities

The programme creates opportunities to build long-term collaborations with professionals and institutions working in:

  • Heritage conservation
  • Landscape architecture
  • Cultural sustainability
  • Creative industries
  • Urban development
  • Environmental conservation

Workshop Focus Areas

The workshop will explore several important themes related to historic garden conservation.

Key Themes Include:

1. Conservation and Integrated Management

Participants will discuss methods for conserving both:

  • Architectural elements
  • Landscape environments

within historic gardens.

2. Traditional Knowledge and Craftsmanship

The programme emphasizes preserving and revitalizing:

  • Traditional building methods
  • Indigenous horticultural practices
  • Cultural craftsmanship
  • Heritage skills transmission

3. Sustainability and Climate Resilience

Projects focused on:

  • Climate adaptation
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Sustainable resource management
  • Ecological restoration

are strongly encouraged.

4. Public Engagement and Creative Activation

The programme supports innovative strategies that make historic gardens more accessible and engaging for modern audiences.

Examples include:

  • Cultural programming
  • Art installations
  • Educational activities
  • Community engagement initiatives
  • Digital storytelling
  • Interactive exhibitions

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from young professionals and practitioners working in heritage conservation and related creative fields.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Conservation architects
  • Landscape architects
  • Garden designers
  • Traditional building engineers
  • Heritage site managers
  • Cultural landscape practitioners
  • Creative industry professionals
  • Researchers and academics
  • Cultural programmers
  • Designers working on heritage projects

Eligible Projects

Applicants may submit projects related to historic gardens and cultural landscapes.

Examples include:

1. Conservation and Restoration Projects

Projects involving:

  • Garden restoration
  • Preservation of historic landscapes
  • Architectural conservation
  • Cultural landscape management

2. Traditional Knowledge Preservation

Projects integrating:

  • Traditional craftsmanship
  • Indigenous horticultural techniques
  • Sustainable garden practices
  • Traditional ecological knowledge

3. Climate and Biodiversity Projects

Projects promoting:

  • Climate resilience
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Ecological regeneration

4. Community Engagement Projects

Initiatives focused on:

  • Public participation
  • Educational outreach
  • Heritage awareness
  • Community stewardship

5. Creative and Artistic Projects

Projects linking historic gardens with:

  • Art
  • Design
  • Digital storytelling
  • Cultural events
  • Creative interpretation

Important Eligibility Conditions

Projects must:

  • Be completed within the last 10 years
  • Demonstrate measurable impact
  • Show innovation and sustainability
  • Reflect cultural relevance
  • Promote public engagement

Projects do not need to be located within UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on both applicant qualifications and project impact.

Candidate Evaluation Criteria

Applicants should demonstrate:

  1. Commitment to cultural dialogue and international cooperation
  2. Leadership in heritage conservation
  3. Innovation and sustainability
  4. Willingness to contribute to peer learning and collaboration

Project Evaluation Criteria

Projects will be assessed based on:

1. Integration of Culture and Nature

Projects should align with sustainable development and environmental goals.

2. Conservation of Traditional Knowledge

Projects should preserve and transmit cultural practices and heritage skills.

3. Innovation and Creativity

Applications should demonstrate creative conservation approaches and modern solutions.

4. Social Impact

Projects should encourage community participation and public engagement.

5. Replicability

Projects should have potential to inspire similar initiatives elsewhere.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Completed Young Masters Application Form
  2. Project description with images (PDF format)
  3. Recommendation letter (optional but encouraged)
  4. Supporting materials such as:
    • Drawings
    • Photos
    • Research papers
    • Reports
    • Videos
  5. Relevant certifications or awards (if available)

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Application Timeline

Key Dates

  • Applications Open: May 1, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 30, 2026
  • Evaluation Period: July 1–15, 2026
  • Results Announcement: July 16–22, 2026
  • Workshop in Suzhou, China: Late August/Early September 2026

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email to:

jgzx_gjjl@hotmail.com

Applicants are advised to carefully prepare their project materials and ensure all supporting documentation is included before submission.

Why You Should Apply

The UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation programme is a rare opportunity for emerging heritage leaders to gain international recognition while contributing to the future of sustainable cultural preservation.

This programme is ideal for young professionals seeking to:

  • Build global networks
  • Showcase impactful conservation work
  • Gain UNESCO recognition
  • Learn from experts
  • Promote sustainable heritage practices
  • Strengthen professional visibility
  • Contribute to global cultural dialogue

As climate change, urbanization, and modernization continue to threaten cultural landscapes, innovative youth-led conservation initiatives are becoming increasingly important worldwide.

Pro Tip for Applicants

Focus your application on measurable impact and storytelling.

UNESCO is looking for projects that not only preserve heritage but also:

  • Engage communities
  • Address sustainability challenges
  • Demonstrate innovation
  • Bridge tradition with modern solutions

Use strong visuals, clear outcomes, and concise narratives to showcase the uniqueness of your work.

Click HERE to learn more about UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China.

Final Thoughts

The UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme offers more than a workshop—it is a global platform for emerging leaders shaping the future of cultural and environmental sustainability.

If you are passionate about historic gardens, cultural landscapes, architecture, sustainability, and public engagement, this is an opportunity to showcase your work on an international stage while connecting with some of the world’s leading heritage professionals.

For young architects, designers, conservation practitioners, and cultural innovators, this programme can become a transformative milestone in your professional journey.

Discover more global Fellowship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY.

The post UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Anna Lindh Foundation Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027 in Egypt: Paid International Opportunity for Young Graduates from 44 Countries
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Anna Lindh Foundation (ALF) has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027, an exceptional international professional development opportunity designed for talented young graduates from across the Euro-Mediterranean region. Hosted at the ALF Secretariat in Alexandria, Egypt, this full-time programme provides selected participants with up to 11 months of professional […]

The post Anna Lindh Foundation Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027 in Egypt: Paid International Opportunity for Young Graduates from 44 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Anna Lindh Foundation (ALF) has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027, an exceptional international professional development opportunity designed for talented young graduates from across the Euro-Mediterranean region.

Hosted at the ALF Secretariat in Alexandria, Egypt, this full-time programme provides selected participants with up to 11 months of professional experience in intercultural dialogue, civil society engagement, public policy, communication, and programme coordination within one of the region’s leading intergovernmental institutions.

The programme is specifically designed for young professionals under the age of 30 who are eager to strengthen their intercultural expertise, gain hands-on international work experience, and contribute to meaningful dialogue and cooperation across Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East.

This opportunity is ideal for graduates seeking international exposure, professional growth, multicultural collaboration, and career advancement in fields related to youth empowerment, intercultural dialogue, policy, communication, and civil society development.

About the Anna Lindh Foundation (ALF)

The Anna Lindh Foundation is an intergovernmental organization dedicated to promoting intercultural dialogue, mutual understanding, and cooperation across the Euro-Mediterranean region.

The Foundation works with an extensive network of civil society organizations, institutions, experts, youth leaders, and stakeholders across 43 countries to strengthen dialogue, inclusion, peacebuilding, and regional collaboration.

The ALF Secretariat, based in Alexandria, Egypt, coordinates programmes, initiatives, and partnerships that empower youth and civil society actors while fostering cultural understanding and cooperation.

Overview of the Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme

The ALF Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme is a structured full-time professional experience designed for recent graduates at the beginning of their careers.

The programme offers selected candidates the opportunity to:

  • Gain professional experience within an international institution
  • Work in a multicultural and multilingual environment
  • Develop practical and intercultural skills
  • Contribute to regional dialogue initiatives
  • Expand their professional networks
  • Improve their competitiveness in the global job market

The programme combines professional training, practical work experience, networking, and intercultural engagement.

Selected participants will begin their assignments on 1 September 2026 at the ALF Secretariat in Alexandria, Egypt.

What the Programme Offers

Participants selected for the programme will benefit from extensive professional and intercultural development opportunities.

Key Benefits of the Programme

1. International Professional Experience

Participants will gain firsthand exposure to the daily operations of the Anna Lindh Foundation Secretariat and develop practical experience in intercultural cooperation and institutional work.

2. Multicultural and Multilingual Environment

Young professionals will work alongside colleagues, experts, and stakeholders from diverse cultural and national backgrounds across the Euro-Mediterranean region.

3. Professional Development

The programme equips participants with valuable skills required in international organizations, civil society institutions, and global professional environments.

4. Networking Opportunities

Participants will build relationships with:

  • International experts
  • Civil society leaders
  • Policy professionals
  • Youth organizations
  • Intercultural dialogue practitioners

5. Career Advancement

The experience gained through the programme significantly enhances employability and competitiveness in international development, diplomacy, public policy, communications, and civil society careers.

Programme Fields Available

The 2026–2027 edition of the programme offers opportunities in four professional fields.

1. Civil Society

Participants in this field will support the activities of the Civil Society Unit and contribute to engagement with more than 4,700 grassroots members across the Euro-Mediterranean region.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Supporting intercultural activities
  • Coordinating with ALF National Civil Society Networks
  • Assisting with online coordination meetings
  • Communicating with stakeholders
  • Monitoring activity implementation timelines
  • Managing documentation and data
  • Supporting intercultural dialogue initiatives

2. Public Policies

Participants will contribute to managing the editorial lifecycle of ALF publications and policy-related materials.

Key tasks include:

  • Communicating with authors and peer reviewers
  • Supporting publishing workflows
  • Copyediting and proofreading
  • Maintaining editorial quality standards
  • Managing administrative and tracking systems
  • Coordinating with Secretariat units

3. Corporate Communication

This field focuses on visibility, storytelling, outreach, and digital communication activities.

Participants may:

  • Develop engaging communication content
  • Support outreach campaigns
  • Promote ALF initiatives online
  • Contribute to storytelling projects
  • Highlight grassroots impact stories
  • Assist with social media and communication coordination
  • Support dissemination of knowledge resources

4. Operations and Coordination

Participants in this area will support the implementation of ALF programmes, events, and initiatives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Assisting with event preparation
  • Supporting logistical coordination
  • Communicating with stakeholders and participants
  • Managing administrative processes
  • Supporting project implementation
  • Contributing to operational activities across different programme phases

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility conditions to be considered for the programme.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be nationals of one of the 44 Union for the Mediterranean (UfM) countries.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be under 30 years old by the anticipated contract start date:

  • 1 September 2026

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree relevant to the selected field
  • A Master’s degree relevant to the selected field

Language Requirement

Candidates must demonstrate:

  • Excellent written and spoken English skills
  • Minimum English proficiency level of C1

Professional Background

Applicants should have:

  • Proven experience or background related to the chosen professional field
  • Strong motivation for intercultural work and regional cooperation

Important Selection Information

The Anna Lindh Foundation notes that selected participants may be assigned to fields based on:

  • Academic background
  • Previous experience
  • Motivation
  • Secretariat operational needs

Assignments may differ from the applicant’s first or second field preference.

Additionally, participants may rotate or contribute to multiple fields according to the needs of the ALF Secretariat.

Application Process

Interested candidates must complete the official online application form in English.

Steps to Apply

  1. Read the full programme call carefully
  2. Prepare all required information and supporting materials
  3. Complete the online application form in English
  4. Submit the application before the deadline

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the ALF Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027 is:

27 June 2026 at 23:59 Central European Time (CET)

Late applications will not be accepted.

Why You Should Apply

The ALF Young Professionals Programme represents a unique gateway into international professional development, intercultural cooperation, and regional dialogue.

Participants gain:

  • International work experience
  • Professional training
  • Practical operational skills
  • Access to regional networks
  • Exposure to multicultural collaboration
  • Enhanced employability

For graduates seeking careers in international organizations, diplomacy, youth engagement, communications, civil society, or policy development, this programme provides an outstanding foundation for long-term success.

Official Website

For complete programme details and application access, visit the official Anna Lindh Foundation website:

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

For more information about this opportunity, Click here 

  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Anna Lindh Foundation Euro-Mediterranean Young Professionals Programme 2026–2027 in Egypt: Paid International Opportunity for Young Graduates from 44 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) has officially opened applications for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium. This prestigious international job opportunity offers qualified professionals the chance to work at the center of global security, defence innovation, and multinational cooperation. The full-time role is housed within the […]

The post The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) has officially opened applications for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium. This prestigious international job opportunity offers qualified professionals the chance to work at the center of global security, defence innovation, and multinational cooperation.

The full-time role is housed within the NATO International Staff (NATO IS) under the NATO Office of Resources (NOR), specifically in the Air & Space Capabilities (ASC) Unit. Successful candidates will contribute directly to NATO’s defence and missile protection strategies while collaborating with experts from member nations and international agencies.

With a highly competitive monthly salary of €8,273.12 and strong international career growth opportunities, this NATO job opening is one of the top defence and security sector opportunities available in 2026.

Overview of the NATO BMD Capabilities Officer Position

Position Details

  • Position Title: Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities
  • Job ID: 260804
  • Organization: NATO International Staff (NATO IS)
  • Location: Brussels, Belgium
  • Employment Type: Full-time
  • Salary: €8,273.12 per month
  • Grade Level: NATO Grade G17-G20
  • Application Deadline: 15 June 2026
  • Contract Duration: Initial 3-year contract with possible renewal up to 6 years

This role is particularly important within NATO’s strategic defence framework, as ballistic missile threats continue to evolve globally. The selected officer will support the development, implementation, evaluation, and lifecycle management of NATO-funded missile defence capabilities.

About NATO’s Office of Resources (NOR)

The NATO Office of Resources serves as a key advisory body within NATO. It provides:

  • Technical advice
  • Financial analysis
  • Economic assessments
  • Policy guidance
  • Resource allocation expertise

The Office supports NATO leadership, Allies, military authorities, and resource committees in managing military common-funded capabilities effectively.

Within this structure, the Officer for BMD Capabilities plays a critical role in evaluating military requirements, investment planning, operational costs, and workforce implications associated with missile defence systems.

Key Responsibilities of the Officer, BMD Capabilities

Successful applicants will be responsible for a broad range of strategic and technical duties.

1. Technical and Strategic Expertise

The officer will:

  • Evaluate NATO military requirements related to missile defence
  • Assess technical solutions for defence projects
  • Analyze project schedules, performance, costs, and risks
  • Review operation and maintenance budgets
  • Monitor NATO-funded capabilities throughout their lifecycle

2. Policy Development

The role includes contributing to NATO policy frameworks by:

  • Supporting policy development for military common-funded resources
  • Applying NATO implementation and operational policies
  • Advising on resource utilization and programme compliance

3. Stakeholder Coordination

The selected candidate will coordinate with:

  • NATO agencies
  • National defence authorities
  • NATO military authorities
  • International Staff members
  • Resource Committees

The position requires strong diplomatic and organizational skills due to the multinational nature of NATO operations.

4. Representation and Leadership

The officer will:

  • Present technical reports to NATO committees
  • Chair technical meetings
  • Lead project discussions involving multiple stakeholders
  • Represent NATO’s Office of Resources in strategic defence meetings

Salary and Benefits

The NATO Officer, BMD Capabilities position offers an attractive compensation package and international work environment.

Financial Benefits

  • Monthly salary of €8,273.12
  • NATO salary scale benefits and allowances
  • International employment benefits package

Professional Benefits

  • Opportunity to work within a globally respected international organization
  • Exposure to high-level defence and security projects
  • Multinational networking opportunities
  • Participation in strategic defence policy development
  • Career advancement within NATO structures

Work-Life Benefits

NATO also offers:

  • Teleworking arrangements
  • Flexible work schedules (Flexitime)
  • Inclusive and diverse work environment
  • Professional development opportunities

Required Qualifications and Experience

To be eligible for this NATO job opportunity, candidates must meet several educational and professional requirements.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent qualification in:
    • Computer Science
    • Electronic Engineering
    • Closely related science or engineering disciplines

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • At least 5 years of professional experience in:
    • Development of complex systems
    • Procurement programmes
    • Maintenance of defence or technical systems
    • Related technical programme management fields

Technical Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Knowledge of project and programme management methodologies
  • Ability to communicate technical and financial information effectively
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

Language Requirements

Candidates must possess NATO language proficiency levels:

  • Advanced level in English or French
  • Elementary level in the second official NATO language

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, NATO considers the following an advantage:

  • Experience with Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD)
  • Knowledge of Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) systems
  • Understanding of NATO common-funding procedures
  • Familiarity with:
    • Space defence operations
    • Air Surveillance systems
    • Counter-Unmanned Air Systems (C-UAS)

Competencies NATO Is Looking For

NATO seeks professionals who can demonstrate:

  • Analytical thinking
  • Leadership and initiative
  • Team collaboration
  • Organizational awareness
  • Strategic influence
  • High performance standards

These competencies are critical for navigating NATO’s complex multinational working environment.

Contract Details and Career Progression

The successful applicant will initially receive:

  • A 3-year definite duration contract
  • Possibility of renewal for another 3 years

During the renewal period, candidates may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract under NATO policies.

Applicants already seconded from national administrations may also be considered under NATO secondment rules.

Diversity and Inclusion at NATO

NATO strongly promotes workplace diversity and equal opportunity employment.

The organization encourages applications from:

  • Women professionals
  • Candidates from all NATO member nations
  • Individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds
  • Applicants living with disabilities

NATO also provides reasonable accommodation during recruitment and employment processes.

Important Application Information

Applicants must submit their applications through NATO’s official recruitment system.

Before Applying

Candidates should ensure:

  • Their CV is updated
  • Supporting documents are accurate
  • Their Taleo candidate profile is complete

Security and Verification

Successful applicants will undergo:

  • Security clearance checks
  • Medical evaluation
  • Qualification verification
  • Accreditation procedures

Important NATO Recruitment Notice

NATO strictly prohibits the use of plagiarism or generative AI tools in preparing applications.

Applications generated wholly or partially using AI systems, translation tools, or copied materials without proper citation may be rejected automatically.

Applicants are advised to prepare authentic and original applications.

Why This NATO Job Opportunity Matters

This position provides an exceptional opportunity for professionals interested in:

  • International defence cooperation
  • Missile defence systems
  • Security policy
  • Strategic military capabilities
  • Aerospace and defence engineering

Working with NATO places professionals at the forefront of international peace, defence innovation, and multinational collaboration.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the NATO Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position is:

15 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all eligibility and documentation requirements before submission.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via NATO’s official recruitment platform.

Apply Here

Applicants should carefully follow all instructions provided on the NATO Talent Acquisition Platform.

 

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

 

The post The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The environmental sector continues to face significant challenges around racial representation and equitable leadership pathways. Despite increasing awareness about diversity and inclusion, leadership roles within environmental organisations still do not reflect the diversity of wider society. To help address this systemic challenge, Common Purpose and Makani Cambridge have launched Natural Catalysts, a transformative 12-month leadership […]

The post Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The environmental sector continues to face significant challenges around racial representation and equitable leadership pathways. Despite increasing awareness about diversity and inclusion, leadership roles within environmental organisations still do not reflect the diversity of wider society.

To help address this systemic challenge, Common Purpose and Makani Cambridge have launched Natural Catalysts, a transformative 12-month leadership programme designed to reshape how leadership is understood, accessed, practised, and sustained across the environmental sector.

Applications are now open for the Natural Catalysts Programme 2026, an initiative specifically created to support Global Majority Leaders and Senior Leaders working together to create lasting systemic change.

The programme aims to build more equitable, inclusive, and sustainable leadership pathways across environmental organisations in the UK.

What Is the Natural Catalysts Programme?

Natural Catalysts is a leadership development initiative focused on:

  • Racial equity
  • Leadership transformation
  • Organisational change
  • Systems thinking
  • Inclusive environmental leadership

The programme is delivered through a partnership between:

  • Common Purpose
  • Makani Cambridge

It is supported by:

  • Esmée Fairbairn Foundation

Unlike many leadership initiatives that focus solely on individual career progression, Natural Catalysts adopts an ecosystem approach.

This means the programme seeks to influence:

  • Individuals
  • Organisations
  • Leadership structures
  • Sector-wide systems
  • Organisational culture
  • Access to power and decision-making

The programme was developed following a year-long research and co-design process involving:

  • Global Majority Leaders
