  • Senior Leaders
  • Environmental organisations
  • Equity practitioners
  • Community stakeholders

Why the Natural Catalysts Programme Matters

The environmental sector has historically struggled with racial representation in leadership positions.

According to the programme organisers, the issue is not simply about talent shortages or recruitment pipelines. Instead, it is deeply connected to:

  • How leadership is defined
  • How opportunities are distributed
  • Organisational power structures
  • Informal access to networks
  • Visibility and sponsorship
  • Systemic inequities

Natural Catalysts seeks to challenge and transform these structures.

The programme recognises that meaningful and lasting change requires:

  • Organisational commitment
  • Shared accountability
  • Cross-level collaboration
  • Honest conversations about race and power
  • Structural change beyond symbolic representation

Programme Vision and Goals

Natural Catalysts is designed to:

  • Increase equitable leadership opportunities
  • Support Global Majority Leaders
  • Encourage institutional accountability
  • Shift leadership culture in the environmental sector
  • Build long-term systemic change

The programme aims to move organisations:

  • From representation to access and influence
  • From mentoring to sponsorship and visibility
  • From individual progression to collective transformation

Who Can Apply?

Natural Catalysts is open to organisations operating within the environmental sector.

Organisations are invited to nominate a “duo” of participants who will complete the programme together.

Required Participant Structure

1. Global Majority Leader

The nominated Global Majority Leader must:

  • Be in a paid role
  • Have lived experience of racialisation in the UK
  • Identify within communities racialised as Black, Asian, Brown, or Arab in the UK context
  • Be operating at mid-management level or above
  • Be navigating leadership pathways within the environmental sector

2. Senior Leader

The Senior Leader must:

  • Be in a paid senior role
  • Hold significant decision-making power
  • Influence organisational direction
  • Be part of senior management or executive leadership

Examples may include:

  • CEOs
  • Directors
  • Senior Management Team members
  • Executive leaders

The organisers emphasise that leadership titles may vary across organisations. What matters most is:

  • Influence
  • Strategic authority
  • Organisational decision-making power

Important Participation Rule

A Global Majority Leader cannot participate without senior leadership involvement.

However:

  • A Senior Leader may participate independently if their organisation does not currently employ a Global Majority Leader.

This structure is intentional because the programme seeks to create organisational change rather than focusing only on individual leadership development.

Additional Participation Opportunity for HR and DEI Professionals

Organisations may also nominate professionals from:

  • Human Resources (HR)
  • Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI)
  • Learning and Development (L&D)

These participants will join a dedicated:

  • Community of Practice

This additional track helps organisations embed learning into policies, systems, and workplace culture.

What Participants Will Learn

The programme explores critical themes around:

  • Leadership
  • Race
  • Power
  • Equity
  • Organisational systems
  • Community-centred change

Participants will:

  • Deepen understanding of power structures
  • Explore leadership beyond hierarchy
  • Learn across lived experiences and roles
  • Build supportive professional communities
  • Strengthen organisational influence
  • Reflect on accountability and responsibility
  • Develop sustainable leadership practices

Programme Structure

Natural Catalysts is a 12-month programme involving:

  • 40 participants total
  • 20 Global Majority Leaders
  • 20 Senior Leaders

The programme combines:

  • In-person residential learning
  • Online sessions
  • Peer communities
  • Collaborative reflection
  • Leadership development
  • Systems-change approaches

Programme Components

1. Online Onboarding Session

The programme begins with a:

  • Two-hour online onboarding session

Participants will:

  • Build initial connections
  • Understand programme expectations
  • Explore themes of leadership and systemic change

2. Global Majority Leaders Track

This specialised leadership track includes:

In-Person Residential

  • Three-day residential gathering
  • Community-building activities
  • Leadership reflection
  • Collective learning experiences

Community of Practice

  • Ten one-hour sessions
  • Peer support and collaborative learning
  • Shared reflection and sense-making

3. Senior Leaders Track

Senior leaders will engage in:

  • Organisational leadership reflection
  • Power and accountability discussions
  • Influence and systems-change learning
  • Cross-sector collaboration

4. Joint Learning Track

Participants from different leadership levels will learn together through:

  • Shared discussions
  • Collaborative exercises
  • Organisational change exploration
  • Leadership practice reflection

5. HR, DEI, and Leadership Professionals Track

This specialised track supports organisational professionals responsible for:

  • Workplace culture
  • Equity systems
  • Inclusion practices
  • Leadership development frameworks

6. Final In-Person Gathering

The programme concludes with a:

  • 24-hour residential gathering

Participants will:

  • Reflect on learning outcomes
  • Share organisational insights
  • Discuss change implementation
  • Celebrate programme completion

Time Commitment

The programme requires meaningful participation and organisational engagement.

Global Majority Leaders

  • 4 in-person days
  • 18 hours online

Senior Leaders

  • 20 hours online
  • 1 in-person day
  • Shadowing and visibility commitments

HR/DEI Participants

  • 4 hours online

The organisers emphasise that this programme is intended to influence participants’ work directly rather than exist alongside it as a separate activity.

Benefits of Participating

Natural Catalysts offers extensive personal, professional, and organisational benefits.

Leadership Development

Participants strengthen:

  • Strategic leadership skills
  • Systems thinking
  • Cross-boundary leadership
  • Organisational influence

Networking and Community

Participants gain:

  • Access to supportive peer networks
  • Cross-sector relationships
  • Collaborative learning communities

Organisational Impact

Organisations benefit from:

  • More inclusive leadership practices
  • Stronger equity frameworks
  • Improved representation pathways
  • Enhanced organisational reflection

Exposure and Visibility

Participants gain:

  • Increased visibility
  • Sponsorship opportunities
  • Access to influential spaces and networks

Sector-Wide Transformation

The programme contributes to:

  • More equitable environmental leadership
  • Sustainable systems change
  • Greater racial representation in leadership roles

Cost and Financial Support

Programme Fee

  • £500 for organisations able to pay

Bursaries

Bursaries are available.

The organisers state clearly that:

  • Financial limitations should not prevent organisations from applying.

Information Webinar

An information webinar will be held for interested applicants.

Webinar Details

  • Date: 23 June 2026
  • Time: 12:00–13:00 BST
  • Platform: Zoom

The webinar will provide:

  • Programme insights
  • Application guidance
  • Opportunity for questions and discussion

About the Organising Partners

Common Purpose

Common Purpose is an international leadership development organisation founded in 1989.

The organisation:

  • Operates in over 100 cities worldwide
  • Has over 130,000 alumni globally
  • Focuses on cross-boundary leadership development
  • Supports collaborative problem-solving across sectors

Makani Cambridge

Makani Cambridge CIC is a community-rooted organisation focused on:

  • Racial equity
  • Leadership
  • Collective care
  • Community-led systems change

Since 2024, Makani has:

  • Delivered more than 70 programmes and events
  • Reached over 1,000 participants across the UK

Their work centres:

  • Lived experience
  • Community-rooted learning
  • Equity-focused systems change

Deadline for Applications

Midnight UK Time, Sunday 12 July 2026

How to Apply

Interested organisations can learn more and apply through the official Common Purpose platform.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Natural Catalysts Programme represents a significant effort to address long-standing inequities in environmental leadership by moving beyond surface-level diversity initiatives toward meaningful structural change.

Through collaboration between Global Majority Leaders, Senior Leaders, and organisational professionals, the programme seeks to transform how leadership is practised and sustained across the environmental sector.

For organisations committed to racial equity, inclusive leadership, and systems transformation, Natural Catalysts offers a rare opportunity to engage deeply with the realities of power, representation, and organisational change while building lasting networks and practical leadership capacity.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CQUniversity Australia RTP Stipend Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Research Scholarship Worth $38,000 Per Year for Domestic and International Students
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
CQUniversity Australia has officially opened applications for the highly competitive Research Training Program (RTP) Stipend Scholarship 2026, a prestigious fully funded research scholarship supported by the Australian Government. The RTP Stipend Scholarship is designed to support outstanding domestic and international students pursuing a Research Higher Degree (RHD) at CQUniversity Australia. The scholarship provides substantial financial […]

The post CQUniversity Australia RTP Stipend Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Research Scholarship Worth $38,000 Per Year for Domestic and International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
CQUniversity Australia has officially opened applications for the highly competitive Research Training Program (RTP) Stipend Scholarship 2026, a prestigious fully funded research scholarship supported by the Australian Government.

The RTP Stipend Scholarship is designed to support outstanding domestic and international students pursuing a Research Higher Degree (RHD) at CQUniversity Australia. The scholarship provides substantial financial assistance to help students cover their living expenses while conducting advanced research studies in Australia.

With a scholarship value of AUD $38,000 per annum for up to three years, this opportunity offers talented researchers the chance to focus fully on their academic and research goals within one of Australia’s leading regional universities.

The scholarship remains open throughout the year, and applicants are encouraged to begin discussions early with potential supervisors and research teams.

About the CQUniversity RTP Stipend Scholarship

The Research Training Program (RTP) Stipend Scholarship is funded by the Australian Government and administered through CQUniversity Australia.

The scholarship supports students undertaking higher degree research programs by providing a living allowance stipend paid fortnightly through the university payroll system.

In addition to the living stipend, domestic students who receive the RTP Stipend Scholarship will also automatically receive an RTP Fees Offset Scholarship, which fully covers tuition fees during the scholarship period.

This scholarship is part of Australia’s broader initiative to strengthen research excellence, innovation, and academic development across universities.

Scholarship Summary

Scholarship Details

  • Host Institution: CQUniversity Australia
  • Scholarship Type: Fully Funded Research Scholarship
  • Funding Organization: Australian Government
  • Scholarship Value: AUD $38,000 per annum
  • Duration: Up to 3 years
  • Number of Scholarships Available: 5
  • Study Level: Research Higher Degree (RHD)
  • Eligible Applicants: Domestic and International Students
  • Study Location: Australia

Scholarship Benefits

The RTP Stipend Scholarship provides substantial financial and academic support to selected candidates.

Benefits Include:

  1. Annual stipend of AUD $38,000
  2. Financial support for living expenses
  3. Opportunity to study and conduct research in Australia
  4. Access to world-class research facilities and supervision
  5. Automatic RTP Fees Offset Scholarship for domestic students
  6. Tuition fee coverage for eligible domestic students
  7. Long-term research and academic career development opportunities
  8. Exposure to international research networks and collaborations
  9. Fortnightly scholarship payments through the university payroll system
  10. Opportunity to contribute to high-impact research projects

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the RTP Stipend Scholarship, applicants must satisfy the eligibility requirements outlined in the Commonwealth Scholarship Guidelines (Research) 2017.

Eligible Candidates

Applicants must be:

  • Domestic students, or
  • International students enrolled in an accredited Research Higher Degree course at CQUniversity

Income Restrictions

To remain eligible for the RTP Stipend:

  • Applicants must not receive another source of income supporting general living costs if the income exceeds 75% of the RTP Stipend rate.
  • Income unrelated to the research program is not considered in eligibility assessments.

Tuition Scholarship Restriction

Applicants are not eligible for an RTP Fees Offset Scholarship if they already receive another equivalent Commonwealth scholarship intended to offset research tuition fees.

Selection Criteria

The RTP Stipend Scholarship is highly competitive and awarded based on academic merit and research potential.

CQUniversity uses a Scholarship Assessment Scale consisting of three major performance categories.

Assessment Categories

1. Academic Achievement and Writing Skills (0–20 points)

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Research writing quality
  • Publications and academic outputs
  • Overall academic achievements

2. Quality of Research Project and Alignment with University Priorities (0–15 points)

Assessment focuses on:

  • Research proposal quality
  • Innovation and originality
  • Alignment with CQUniversity research priority areas
  • Potential research impact

3. Motivation, Research Experience, and Research Environment (0–15 points)

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Strong research motivation
  • Previous research experience
  • Capacity to succeed in research training
  • Supportive supervisory and research environment

How to Apply

Unlike many scholarship programs, there is no direct application process for the RTP Scholarship.

Application Process

Step 1: Discuss with a Supervisory Team

Interested applicants must first discuss their research interests and scholarship intentions with a potential supervisory team at CQUniversity.

Step 2: Recommendation by Supervisors

Based on the recommendation of the supervisory team, prospective applicants may then be invited by the School of Graduate Research to submit a formal application.

Step 3: Submit Supporting Documents

Applicants invited to apply must provide all required supporting documentation.

Required Supporting Documents

Applications must include:

  1. Written submission
  2. Supporting academic documents
  3. Any additional documentation requested by CQUniversity

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Scholarship Assessment Rounds

Although applications remain open throughout the year, CQUniversity may conduct multiple scholarship assessment rounds annually depending on scholarship availability.

Typical Assessment Round Deadlines

Assessment rounds are generally scheduled around:

  • End of April
  • End of September

However, the timing and number of rounds may vary from year to year.

Following each assessment round:

  • Applications are reviewed
  • Successful applicants are selected
  • Candidates are notified via email

Important Dates

Scholarship Opening Date

31 December 2025

Scholarship Closing Date

31 December 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin contacting potential supervisors early to improve their chances of consideration.

Why You Should Apply

The CQUniversity RTP Stipend Scholarship is an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded postgraduate education in Australia.

The scholarship not only removes financial barriers but also provides access to:

  • Advanced research training
  • Expert supervision
  • International academic exposure
  • Career development opportunities
  • Global research collaborations

Whether your goal is an academic career, research leadership, innovation, or industry impact, the RTP Scholarship can provide the resources and support necessary to achieve your ambitions.

Official Scholarship Link

For complete scholarship details and official information, visit the CQUniversity Australia scholarship page.

 

There are no direct applications for RTP Scholarships. Please discuss your interest in an RHD Scholarship with your supervisory team. Prospective applicants will be invited by the School of Graduate Research to complete an application, based on the recommendation of the supervisory team.

Depending on the availability of RTP stipend scholarships, CQUniversity may conduct more than one assessment round within the calendar year. These rounds are typically scheduled to close at the end of April and September; however, the timing and number of rounds cannot be guaranteed and may vary from year to year. Applications remain open throughout the year.

Following the close of each assessment round, applications will be reviewed and applicants will be notified of the outcome via email.

Applicants will be notified by email when the scholarships have been awarded.

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post CQUniversity Australia RTP Stipend Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Research Scholarship Worth $38,000 Per Year for Domestic and International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
